title: MCC Bank Identity and PPS Privacy Policy
content: The Mangalore Catholic Co-operative Bank Ltd is a Primary Urban Co-operative Bank in Karnataka with Head Office at Hampankatta, Mangalore - 575001. The PPS Privacy Policy applies to users of the bank's Positive Pay System application. By viewing or using the PPS application, users consent to the collection, use, and disclosure practices stated in the policy.

title: PPS Data Collection, Sharing, and Security
content: Through the PPS application, MCC Bank may collect information voluntarily disclosed at registration, including name, account numbers, mobile number, contact information, transaction information, and PPS usage activity. The PPS application does not gather additional mobile-device information through cookies or similar technologies. MCC Bank does not share this information with other companies unless required by law or while handling disputes. PPS reply messages mask the first few characters of account numbers, and users must type their bank-provided or self-set PIN to authorize access. Users should not share their PIN with anyone.

title: PPS Privacy Policy Updates and Contact
content: The PPS Privacy Policy is effective from 23/05/2024. MCC Bank may update the policy at any time, and changes become effective immediately after being posted. Continued use after changes means the user acknowledges and accepts the modified policy. MCC Bank is not responsible for separately informing each registered customer about privacy-policy changes. Material changes will be prominently mentioned by notice on the authorised website. Privacy questions can be sent to info@mccbank.in.

title: General Banking Services and Contact Channels
content: MCC Bank services include locker facility at all branches, any branch banking, Pigmy deposit at doorstep, SMS alerts for transactions, personalised cheque book printing, Host to Host H2H NEFT/RTGS, and kiosk passbook printing at Founders Branch. NRE account queries can be sent by WhatsApp to 7090701104. Complaints can be emailed to gm@mccbank.in and chairman@mccbank.in, or raised by mobile on 7090701115 and 9845278901.

title: e-Stamp Facility Branches
content: MCC Bank e-Stamp facility is available at Founders, Ashoknagar, Kankanady, Kulshekar, Morgansgate, Shirva, Bajpe, Kinnigoli, Surathkal, Udupi, Puttur, B C Road, Karkala, Brahmavara, Belthangady, Belman, Byndoor, and Santhekatte branches.

title: Recurring Deposit Eligibility and Documents
content: Recurring Deposit accounts can be opened by resident individuals singly or jointly, by a natural guardian such as father or mother on behalf of a minor with date of birth declared, by a guardian appointed by court, and by a Co-operative Society through persons authorised by its Board of Directors. Minimum monthly instalment is Rs. 100. Documents required are identity proof and address proof such as Passport, Aadhaar card, Driving Licence, or Voter ID card, PAN card, and photographs. Aadhaar card is mandatory to open the account.

title: Recurring Deposit Operations and Interest Rates
content: Recurring Deposit monthly instalments must be paid before the end of the month. Standing instructions from Savings Bank or Current accounts for monthly instalments are accepted and effected free of charge until maturity. Each depositor receives a pass book. Nomination facility is available. MCC Bank may grant loans on Recurring Deposit security up to 85% of the balance at credit. RD interest rates are 6.75% for 1 year to less than 2 years and 6.50% for 2 years and above but less than 5 years. Senior citizens get an additional 0.50%.

title: Fixed Deposit Eligibility, Minimum Amount, and Documents
content: Fixed Deposit can be opened by an individual in own name, two or more individuals jointly with repayment instructions, a guardian on behalf of a minor, and in the name of clubs, societies, associations, educational institutions, trusts, firms, partnership concerns, joint stock companies, executors, and administrators. Nomination facility is available. Minimum initial deposit is Rs. 100. Documents required are identity proof and address proof such as Passport, Aadhaar card, Driving Licence, or Voter ID card, PAN card, and photographs. Aadhaar card is mandatory.

title: Fixed Deposit Interest Rates Effective 02-04-2026
content: MCC Bank Fixed Deposit interest rates are: 15 days to 45 days - 4.00%; 46 days to 90 days - 4.75%; 91 days to 180 days - 5.25%; 181 days to 364 days - 6.00%; 1 year to less than 2 years - 6.75%; 2 years and above but less than 5 years - 6.50%; 456 days Fixed Deposit w.e.f. 02-04-2026 - 7.00%; 456 days Fixed Deposit Senior Citizen w.e.f. 02-04-2026 - 7.50%. Senior citizens get additional 0.50%. Co-operative Societies and Banks get additional 0.50% only for 456 days and deposits above Rs 25,00,000.

title: Fixed Deposit Maturity, Premature Closure, Renewal, Loan, and Transfer
content: Fixed Deposit is repayable on the maturity date shown on the deposit receipt. For premature closure, interest is paid at 1% less than the rate applicable for the period the deposit remained with the bank. In the event of the depositor's death, the 1% interest deduction is not applicable for premature payment. Matured deposits can be renewed fully or partly from maturity date to a future date if renewal is for at least 15 days beyond renewal date and is made within 15 days from maturity. Loan against deposit is available up to 85% of deposit amount. Loan interest is 1% higher than deposit interest for Rs 25 Lakhs and above, and 2% higher for loan amount less than Rs 25 Lakhs. Fixed Deposit can be transferred from one branch to another only on maturity.

title: Deposit and Account Service Charges Effective 01-10-2024
content: As per MCC Bank service charges w.e.f. 01-10-2024: Savings account cheque book has no charges up to 20 leaves per calendar year, then Rs 4 per leaf. Current Account or CCL cheque book is Rs 5 per leaf. Ledger folio charge for Current Account or CCL is Rs 50 per page per calendar year, treating 40 entries as a page or part. Statement of Account where pass book is issued is Rs 20 per page. Duplicate SOA or pass book with latest entry is Rs 100; with earlier old entries is Rs 100 plus Rs 40 per page. Account closure within one year is Rs 200 for Savings or available balance whichever is lower, and Rs 300 for Current or available balance whichever is lower. Non-maintenance of minimum average balance is Rs 100 per quarter for Savings and Rs 250 per quarter for Current; minimum quarterly average balance is Rs 1000 for Savings and Rs 3000 for Current.

title: Stop Payment, Standing Instruction, and Account Certificates Charges
content: MCC Bank stop payment charges w.e.f. 01-10-2024: noting stop payment against cheque issued is Rs 250 per instance for Savings Bank and Rs 500 for Current Account or CCL. Stop payment for loss of cheque book is Rs 250 per instance for Savings and Rs 500 for Current Account or CCL. Revoking stop payment is Rs 100 per instance for SB/CA/CCL. Registering standing instruction is Rs 200 excluding interest on deposits, RD instalment, and loan instalment. Standing instruction funds transfer to other banks is Rs 50 per transaction plus applicable DD/NEFT/RTGS charges; transfer within the bank is Rs 20 per transaction. Duplicate deposit receipt is Rs 250. Balance certificate, account maintaining certificate, or cheque honoured certificate is Rs 150 per instance. Photo attestation is Rs 500. Addition or deletion of names in SB deposits is Rs 250 per instance, waived for Associations and Institutions. Nomination modification or change is NIL. Dormant account activation is NIL.

title: DD, NEFT, RTGS, and Collection Service Charges
content: MCC Bank remittance and collection charges w.e.f. 01-10-2024 include DD/PO issue up to Rs 10,000 at Rs 50 per instrument; above Rs 10,000 at Rs 5 per thousand per instrument, minimum Rs 100 and maximum Rs 5000. DD/PO cancellation is Rs 250 or actual amount of DD/PO whichever is lower per instrument. DD/PO revalidation is Rs 100 per instrument. RTGS charges are Rs 25 for Rs 2,00,000 to Rs 5,00,000 and Rs 50 above Rs 5,00,000. No NEFT/RTGS charges apply for customers maintaining average balance of Rs 1,00,000 and above in SB/CA. Outstation cheque collection charges are Rs 50 up to Rs 10,000, Rs 100 above Rs 10,000 to Rs 1,00,000, Rs 500 above Rs 1,00,000 to Rs 10,00,000, and Rs 1000 above Rs 10,00,000. Charges up to Rs 1,00,000 are mandated by RBI and inclusive of out-of-pocket expenses such as post/courier charges.

title: Cheque Return and Bill Return Service Charges
content: MCC Bank inward cheque return or ECS return charges for cheques drawn on the bank are Rs 100 up to Rs 5,00,000 and Rs 300 above Rs 5,00,000 for SB/CA/CCL. No charge applies if returned for technical reasons, not at customer fault, or where stop payment is noted. Outward cheque returns are Rs 200 for Savings Bank and Rs 300 for CA/CCL. Inward or outward bills returned under IBC/OBC are Rs 500 per bill.

title: Loan Processing and Upfront Service Charges
content: MCC Bank service charges w.e.f. 01-10-2024: Upfront fee on term loans except CCL, loan to vehicles, Jewel Loan, and Loan against Deposit is 1.00% of loan amount with minimum Rs 2000 up to Rs 20 lakhs, and 0.50% of entire loan amount with minimum Rs 20000 and maximum Rs 400000 above Rs 20 lakhs. For transport operators, upfront fee is 1.00% up to Rs 75 lakhs with minimum Rs 2000, and 0.50% above Rs 75 lakhs with minimum Rs 75000 and maximum Rs 400000. Education loan upfront fee is 0.50% of loan amount with minimum Rs 2000. Private vehicle and MCL upfront fee is 1% for two-wheeler with minimum Rs 1000, 0.50% for four-wheeler with minimum Rs 1000, and 1% for MCL with minimum Rs 500.

title: CCL, Loan Revalidation, Preclosure, and Documentation Charges
content: MCC Bank CCL processing charges w.e.f. 01-10-2024: new limit is 0.75% of limit with minimum Rs 2000 and maximum Rs 400000; renewal without enhancement is 0.50% of limit with minimum Rs 1000 and maximum Rs 250000; renewal with enhancement is 0.50% on existing limit plus 0.75% on additional limit, all-in-all minimum Rs 2000 and maximum Rs 400000. Loan sanction revalidation is Rs 1500 irrespective of loan type and amount. Loan preclosure other than takeover by other financial institutions, for loans closed before 1 year, is 2% of outstanding liability or drawing power whichever is higher. Preclosure by takeover by other FIs is 4% of outstanding liability or drawing power whichever is higher for all loans, and/or sanctioned CCL limit. Loan documentation charges are Rs 1500 per mortgage where mortgage is involved, Rs 750 per loan where mortgage is not involved, and Rs 200 per MCL or other unsecured loan.

title: Loan Reports, Notices, Certificates, and Safe Custody Charges
content: MCC Bank loan-related charges include CIC report Rs 100 for Housing Loan, Education Loan, CCL, and Private Vehicle Loan; Rs 250 for all other loans including CCL renewal; and Rs 500 for Commercial CIC. CERSAI search report is Rs 50, registration or modification of charge is Rs 100, and satisfaction of charge is NIL. Release of security or change in sanction terms is Rs 1000 per instance plus legal opinion and valuation charges where applicable. Jewel Loan service charge is 0.50% of loan amount up to Rs 4,00,000 with minimum Rs 250; above Rs 4,00,000 is NIL. Loan notice by RPAD/courier is Rs 100 per packet or actual charges whichever is higher; ordinary post is Rs 50 per packet or actual charges whichever is higher. Certified copies of loan documents are Rs 30 per document. No Due or No Objection Certificate is Rs 250 per instance. Loan balance certificate to other bank is Rs 100 per instance. Safe custody charge is Rs 100 per month or part for mortgage documents or jewels not taken within one month of loan closure.

title: Locker Rent and Locker Service Charges Effective 01-04-2025
content: MCC Bank locker and other service charges w.e.f. 01-04-2025: small locker size up to 0.50 cubic feet rent Rs 1250; medium locker size 0.51 to 1.50 cubic feet rent Rs 2000; large locker size 1.51 to 2.50 cubic feet rent Rs 3500; extra large locker size above 2.50 cubic feet rent Rs 5000. Loss of locker key costs Rs 1500 plus actual break open charges. Up to 12 locker operations per year are NIL; above 12 operations, Rs 50 per operation. Penal charges on delayed annual locker rent are 10% of rent up to first quarter, 20% up to second quarter, 30% up to third quarter, 40% up to fourth quarter, and 50% for 1 year and above.

title: Cash, ATM Card, GST, and Service Charge Notes
content: MCC Bank cash handling charges are NIL. ATM card annual fee is Rs 150 per card, additional card is Rs 100 per card, replacement card is Rs 250 per card, and replacement PIN is Rs 50 per PIN. GST is applicable on all types of service charge and must be collected in addition to the service charge; the present GST rate shown in the service-charge document is 18%. Total amount should be rounded off to the nearest rupee. There are no charges on loan against deposit. Staff are exempt from service charges other than locker rent.

title: Savings Bank Account Eligibility, Opening Amount, and Documents
content: MCC Bank Savings Bank Account can be opened by an individual in own name, two or more individuals jointly with suitable repayment instructions, a guardian on behalf of a minor, and clubs, societies, associations, educational institutions, trusts, firms, partnership concerns, executors and administrators. Secretaries, treasurers, managers, or other duly constituted officers of corporations may open accounts after furnishing rules and required information in writing. Nomination facility is available. Initial deposit is minimum Rs. 1000. Minimum amount for cheque book facility is Rs. 1000. Required documents are identity proof and address proof such as Passport, Aadhaar card, Driving Licence, or Voter ID card, PAN card, and photographs. Aadhaar card is mandatory.

title: Savings Bank Interest, Facilities, Passbook, and Minimum Balance
content: MCC Bank Savings Bank Account interest rate is 3.00%. SSB account maintaining above Rs. 1,00,000 gets 5.00%. Interest is paid at rates applicable for the period of deposit as prescribed under Reserve Bank of India rules, directives, and guidelines from time to time and fixed by the Bank. Cheque book facility is provided to account holders maintaining minimum balance of Rs. 1000. First CTS cheque book is free of cost and subsequent cheque book is charged. Passbook issued on account opening has no charge. Duplicate passbook is issued on payment of applicable charges. NEFT/RTGS and Demand Draft facilities are available. Non-maintenance of minimum balance will be charged.

title: Education Loan Purpose, Rate, Tenure, Security, and Terms
content: MCC Bank Education Loan is for higher studies in India and abroad. Maximum repayment tenure is 15 years including moratorium period. Borrower is the student along with parent. Interest rate is 10.50% with immediate effect irrespective of percentage of collateral security. Minimum one guarantor with sufficient net worth is required. Security is mortgage of unencumbered immovable property belonging to the applicant situated within the jurisdiction of the Bank; in direct finance it should be within the territorial jurisdiction of the branch where the loan is availed. Minimum margin is 15%. Membership is necessary for borrower and sureties. Rate is floating. Minimum CIBIL score is 600.

title: MSME Business and CCL Loan Interest, Tenure, Security, and Margin
content: MCC Bank MSME Loan eligibility and purpose cover any activity defined under MSME. MSME Business Loan rates for businessmen and industrialists are: CIBIL 700 and above - 11.50%; 600 to 699 - 12.00%; (-1) or up to 100 as per new norms - 11.50%; above 100 up to 200 as per new norms - 12.00%. MSME CCL working capital rates are: 700 and above - 11.00%; 600 to 699 - 12.00%; (-1) or up to 100 as per new norms - 11.00%; above 100 up to 200 as per new norms - 12.00%. Repayment tenure is up to 120 months in equated monthly instalments. Minimum one guarantor with sufficient net worth is required. Security is any tangible asset belonging to the applicant situated within bank jurisdiction; for direct finance, within branch territorial jurisdiction. Margin is at least 25% of total project cost. Membership is necessary for borrower and sureties. Rates are floating.

title: MSME Transport Operator and Machinery Loan Rates
content: MCC Bank MSME Transport Operator rates: new Light Motor Vehicle 700 and above - 11.50%, 600 to 699 - 12.50%, (-1) or up to 100 - 11.50%, above 100 up to 200 - 12.50%; new Heavy Motor Vehicle 700 and above - 12.50%, 600 to 699 - 13.50%, (-1) or up to 100 - 12.50%, above 100 up to 200 - 13.50%; used commercial LMV - 13.00%, used commercial heavy vehicle - 14.00%, boat repair or vehicle repair - 13.50%. Repayment tenure is up to 120 months, with minimum one guarantor, tangible asset security, at least 25% project-cost margin, and membership for borrower and sureties. Hypothecation Loan on Machinery covers MSME machinery purchase. New plant and machinery up to Rs 25 lakhs is 11.50%; above Rs 25 lakhs is 12.00% for 700 and above, 12.50% for 600 to 699, 11.00% for (-1) up to 100, and 12.50% for above 100 up to 200. Old unit purchase or takeover for CIBIL 600 and above is 12.00% up to Rs 50 lakhs and 12.00% above Rs 50 lakhs. Margin is 25% of invoice or quotation for new machinery and 50% of valuation for used machinery. Machinery repayment tenure is up to 7 years.

title: Agriculture Allied Activity Loan Rates
content: MCC Bank agriculture and allied activity loan rates are: Boat Loans - 13.00%; Boat Loans used or repair - 14.00%; Poultry, Piggery, Dairy and other allied activities - 11.00%; Commercial Crop Loans - 11.00%. Rates are floating.

title: Jewel Loan Eligibility, Rate, Margin, and Terms
content: MCC Bank Jewel Loan eligibility covers any person with assured monthly income acceptable to the Bank. Gold loan is granted to Associate and Regular Members at the discretion of the Branch Manager or authorised officer. The borrower should be a good and regular customer or introduced as per KYC norms. Jewel Loan ROI is 10.75% per annum for priority Agricultural and MSME, and 10.75% for non-priority up to Rs. 4,00,000 for 1 year and above Rs. 4,00,000. Loan amount is based on market price. Maximum permissible loan is 75% of the 30 days average market value. Membership is necessary. No processing charges for Jewel Loans above Rs 4 lakhs repayable in monthly instalments, but Jewel Appraiser fees are charged. Minimum CIC score is 600.

title: Surety and Consumer Loan Rate, Limit, Purpose, and Terms
content: MCC Bank Surety Loan and Consumer Loan eligibility is any person with assured monthly income. Interest rate is 15.00% floating. Repayment tenure is up to 36 months. Maximum loan limit is Rs. 1,00,000. Purpose includes purchase of household articles or furniture, house repairs or renovation, marriage, and similar needs. Minimum one guarantor with sufficient net worth is required. Security is hypothecation of purchased items out of bank finance and any other acceptable movable item. Margin is 25% of quotation amount. Membership is necessary for borrower and sureties. Minimum CIBIL score is 600.

title: Private Vehicle Loan New and Used Vehicle Terms
content: MCC Bank Private Vehicle Loan eligibility is any person with assured monthly income acceptable to the Bank. New vehicle loan purpose is purchase of new vehicle. Used vehicle loan purpose is purchase of used vehicle below 5 years. New four-wheeler rates are: CIBIL 650 and above - 10.00%; 600 to 649 - 11.00%; (-1) or up to 100 as per new norms - 10.00%; above 100 up to 200 as per new norms - 11.00%. New two-wheeler rate is 15.00%. Used LMV private vehicle below 5 years rates are: model within 2 years - 11.50%; vehicle older than 2 years and less than 5 years - 13.00%. Repayment tenure is up to 7 years for new vehicles and depending on model for used vehicles. Minimum one guarantor is required. Security is new vehicle plus additional security insisted by Bank, or used vehicle plus additional security. Margin is at least 25% of quotation value for two-wheeler and 15% for four-wheeler. Used three/four wheelers and above need 30% of valuation for age up to 3 years and 50% for age above 3 years up to 5 years. Membership is necessary for borrower and sureties.

title: Mortgage Loan Eligibility, Rate, Tenure, Security, and Margin
content: MCC Bank Mortgage Loan eligibility is any person with assured monthly income acceptable to the Bank. Purpose includes repair, renovation, furnishing of house, construction of compound wall, and meeting marriage expenses of self or close relatives. Interest rate is 13.50% floating. Repayment tenure is up to 10 years. Minimum one guarantor with sufficient net worth is required. Security is mortgage of unencumbered immovable property belonging to the applicant. Maximum permissible amount is 60% of property valuation or 75% of estimate, whichever is lower. Membership is necessary for borrower and sureties.

title: Complaints and Human Escalation Contacts
content: For complaints, MCC Bank provides email contacts gm@mccbank.in and chairman@mccbank.in. Mobile contacts listed for complaints are 7090701115 and 9845278901. NRE account queries can be raised over WhatsApp at 7090701104. Privacy questions about PPS can be sent to info@mccbank.in.

title: Current Account Eligibility and Opening Requirements
content: MCC Bank Current Account can be opened by sole proprietors, firms, partnerships, joint stock companies, corporate bodies, trusts, educational and charitable institutions. Limited liability partnerships and NGOs registered under Societies Registration Act are also eligible. Minimum initial deposit is Rs. 5000. Nomination facility is available. Required documents include business registration certificate, partnership deed, incorporation certificate, latest audited financial statements, trade license, and PAN certificate. Individual proprietor must provide identity and address proofs such as Passport, Aadhaar card, or Driving Licence.

title: Current Account Features and Facilities
content: MCC Bank Current Account holders enjoy unlimited cheque book facility, any branch banking across all MCC Bank branches, unlimited deposits and withdrawals without daily limits, quarterly interest-free overdraft facility up to sanctioned limit, standing instructions for fund transfers at no cost, multi-signature authority as per organization requirements, online banking with API integration for corporate clients, and regular account statements. Business hours for deposits vary from 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM, and cash counters close at 2:00 PM on all working days.

title: Current Account Interest Rates and Minimum Balance
content: MCC Bank Current Account interest on deposits is 2.50% per annum on daily balance above Rs. 2 Lakhs. Minimum average balance requirement is Rs. 3000 per calendar quarter. Failure to maintain minimum balance attracts a penalty of Rs. 250 per quarter. Premium Current Accounts maintaining average balance of Rs. 10 Lakhs or above receive preferential rates at 3.75% per annum and waiver of minimum balance penalty. All current account interest is calculated and credited quarterly.

title: Account Overdraft and Cash Credit Limit Facilities
content: MCC Bank provides overdraft facility on Current and Savings accounts based on approved limits. Overdraft limit is determined by bank after creditworthiness assessment and valid collateral security. For Current Accounts, overdraft interest rate is 2% higher than the lending rate for the respective category. For Savings Bank Overdraft, rate is 3% higher than the standard lending rate. Overdraft facility is reviewed annually and can be cancelled with 30 days notice. Overdraft utilization beyond sanctioned limit attracts penal interest at 4% higher than applicable rate. Monthly statement clearly shows overdraft utilization and interest charged.

title: Non-Resident External Account NRE Features and Rules
content: MCC Bank Non-Resident External (NRE) accounts are opened by Indian citizens residing abroad and Non-Resident Indians (NRI). NRE Savings account minimum balance is Rs. 2000. NRE Fixed Deposit minimum investment is Rs. 1000. Funds in NRE accounts must be remitted from abroad in foreign currency through banking channels. NRE deposits are fully repatriable. Interest earned on NRE accounts is exempted from Indian income tax. NRE account holder can nominate a resident Indian to receive funds in case of death. Annual Percentage Yield on NRE deposits depends on tenor and ranges from 4.50% to 7.00%.

title: NRO Account Non-Resident Ordinary Account Guidelines
content: MCC Bank Non-Resident Ordinary (NRO) account is opened by Non-Resident Indians (NRI) to manage locally earned income and local obligations. NRO account can receive remittance from NRE account, local income within India, and foreign inward remittance. NRO deposits are not directly repatriable but interest and dividend income can be repatriated with RBI approval. Minimum balance for NRO Savings Account is Rs. 1000. NRO Fixed Deposit minimum is Rs. 1000. Interest on NRO deposits is taxable as per Indian income tax laws. NRO account operates under Indian income tax rules and local banking regulations.

title: Standing Instruction Setup and Execution Rules
content: MCC Bank Standing Instruction is an automated recurring fund transfer scheduled at specified intervals. Standing Instructions can be set up for fixed amount transfers, variable amount transfers based on formulas, or transfers on specific dates. Frequency options include daily, weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, or annually. Standing Instructions from Savings or Current Account to accounts within MCC Bank are free of charge. Transfers to accounts in other banks via NEFT or RTGS attract applicable charges. Standing Instruction can be modified or cancelled anytime with written request. Failed Standing Instruction due to insufficient balance is re-attempted up to 3 times within 5 working days.

title: Multi-City Cheque Clearing and MCC Clearing House
content: MCC Bank participates in Multi-City Clearing (MCC) House operated under RBI guidelines. Cheques deposited for collection through MCC Clearing are credited within 2-3 working days. Local cheques are cleared within 1 working day in most locations. Cheques drawn on MCC Bank branches are cleared on the same day if deposited before 12:00 PM at collection branch. Collection charges for local cheques are NIL for maintaining average balance of Rs. 1 Lakh and above. Out-of-station cheques are cleared based on RBI guidelines. Return charges apply if cheques are dishonoured due to insufficient funds or other reasons.

title: Demand Draft Issue and Cancellation Procedure
content: MCC Bank issues Demand Drafts (DD) as a safe mode of remittance. DD can be issued in Indian Rupees up to any amount. DD validity is 6 months from the date of issue. DD can be cancelled if unused by presenting the original DD and requesting cancellation at the issuing branch. Cancellation charges are Rs. 100 or actual DD value loss, whichever is lower. Cancelled DD amount is credited back to customer account within 3 working days after deducting cancellation charges. Duplicate DD can be issued if original DD is lost with payment of Rs. 100 duplicate DD charges. DD is payable at par across all MCC Bank branches and authorized centers.

title: RTGS Real Time Gross Settlement Facility and Rates
content: MCC Bank provides Real Time Gross Settlement (RTGS) facility for urgent fund transfers to other bank accounts. RTGS is available for amounts above Rs. 2,00,000. Settlement happens in real-time as soon as receiving bank accepts the payment. RTGS charges are Rs. 25 for amount Rs. 2,00,000 to Rs. 5,00,000 and Rs. 50 for amount above Rs. 5,00,000. RTGS charges are waived for customers maintaining average balance of Rs. 1 Lakh and above in Savings or Current account. RTGS timing is 9:00 AM to 4:30 PM on working days. Failed RTGS payments are reversed to sender's account within 2-3 working days.

title: NEFT National Electronic Funds Transfer System
content: MCC Bank provides National Electronic Funds Transfer (NEFT) facility for non-urgent fund transfers to other bank accounts. NEFT is available for any amount. NEFT operates in hourly batches throughout the day. Credit of NEFT is usually within 1-2 hours. NEFT charges are Rs. 2.50 for amount up to Rs. 10,000 and Rs. 5 for amount above Rs. 10,000. NEFT charges are waived for customers maintaining average balance of Rs. 1 Lakh and above. NEFT is available 6 days a week from 8:00 AM to 7:00 PM. NEFT offers good audit trail for corporate and institutional customers.

title: Mobile Banking Security and OTP Verification
content: MCC Bank mobile banking app requires Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) using OTP (One-Time Password). OTP is sent to registered mobile number for every login attempt and transaction. Mobile Banking PIN must be set during first login and changed regularly. Biometric authentication (fingerprint/face recognition) is available on supported devices for faster access. Session timeout after 15 minutes of inactivity for security. Transaction limit on mobile banking is Rs. 2,00,000 per transaction and Rs. 5,00,000 per day for Savings Account. Mobile banking is available 24x7 on all days. Lost transaction confirmation SMS is sent after every successful transaction.

title: Internet Banking Features and Access Control
content: MCC Bank Internet Banking portal provides online account management accessible 24x7 from any location. Features include fund transfer, bill payment, loan application, deposit renewal, and account statement download. Internet Banking uses SSL encryption for data security. Multi-user access is available for business accounts with role-based permissions. Email and SMS notification alerts are sent for all transactions. Login requires password and OTP. Session logout happens automatically after 30 minutes of inactivity. Regular security updates and patches are deployed automatically. Customer support for internet banking is available through live chat and email.

title: Account Statement Download and Frequency Options
content: MCC Bank account statement can be downloaded from internet banking portal or mobile app in PDF format. Customers can download statements for last 12 months online without any charge. Statement frequency options include daily, weekly, monthly, or quarterly statements. Email delivery of statements can be auto-configured on monthly basis. Physical passbook is provided for all account types with regular updates at branch. Statement clearly shows all transactions, interest credits, and charges applied. Account statement also displays available balance, reserved amount, and any holds on the account.

title: Account Linking and Co-Operative Banking Principles
content: MCC Bank follows co-operative banking principles of member participation, democratic control, and mutual aid. All account holders become associate or regular members based on account type and tenure. Members have voting rights on bank policies and participate in annual general meetings. Member surplus is distributed as dividend at year-end if bank makes profit. Membership is transferable to legal heirs in case of death. Co-operative Banking Regulation Act, 1912 governs MCC Bank operations. Member education programs on banking practices and financial literacy are conducted quarterly at branches.

title: Account Closure and Settlement Procedure
content: MCC Bank account closure requires written request submitted to branch with identification proof. Account must be cleared of all outstanding dues and cheques before closure. Account closure within one year of opening attracts Rs. 200 charge for Savings account and Rs. 300 for Current account or available balance, whichever is lower. Account closure after one year is free. Closing balance along with interest accrued is credited to customer's nominated bank account or issued as cheque. NOC (No Objection Certificate) is issued on request after closure. Membership is automatically terminated on account closure.

title: Dormant Account Activation and Revival Process
content: MCC Bank account is classified as dormant if there is no customer-initiated transaction for 12 consecutive months. Dormant accounts are flagged in the system but funds remain safe. Account activation is free and requires submission of written application with identification proof. Funds in dormant account earn zero interest. Account statements for dormant accounts are available only after reactivation. Dormant account can be reactivated anytime by customer request or visit to branch. All previous cheques issued from dormant account become invalid and new cheque book is issued on activation. Customer receives welcome back SMS or email notification on dormant account activation.

title: Nomination Modification and Succession Planning
content: MCC Bank allows account holders to nominate one or more beneficiaries for account succession. Nomination can be made at account opening or modified anytime later. Nomination modification is free and requires written request with new nominee details and identification proof. Multiple nominees can be designated with specified percentage share. Nomination is applicable on Fixed Deposits, Recurring Deposits, and Savings Accounts. In case of account holder's death, nominee can claim the account amount with original death certificate and legal heirship proof. Bank processes nomination claim within 30 days of receiving all required documents.

title: Account Restrictions and Compliance Requirements
content: MCC Bank implements RBI guidelines for account monitoring and Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance. Accounts with suspicious transaction patterns are flagged for investigation per anti-money laundering guidelines. Customer Due Diligence (CDD) is mandatory for all accounts exceeding Rs. 10 Lakhs annual turnover. Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) is required for high-risk customers as per RBI norms. Account can be restricted if KYC documents are not updated within prescribed timeline. Regular CDD renewal is required every 2 years for existing customers. Non-compliance with KYC requirements may result in account closure without prior notice.

title: GST Registration and Tax Compliance for Businesses
content: MCC Bank requires GST Registration Certificate for business accounts if turnover exceeds Rs. 20 Lakhs per annum. GST registration number must be provided for new business account opening or within 30 days of obtaining GST. Business account statements are required by tax authorities for income tax compliance. MCC Bank provides quarterly business account analysis report showing cash flow and transaction trends. Bank provides tax compliance certificate on request for audit purposes. Business accounts need to file quarterly return of sorts (RoS) certificate with bank. Bank freezes account if GST tax dues are pending as per tax department notification.

title: Savings Bank Account Interest Calculation and Crediting
content: MCC Bank Savings Account interest is calculated on daily balance basis. Interest is computed at 3.00% per annum for regular Savings Account and 5.00% for Savings Account maintaining above Rs. 1,00,000. Super Saver Account earns 6.00% interest on deposits above Rs. 5,00,000. Interest calculation considers lowest balance between 10th and last day of calendar month. Interest is credited to account on last day of calendar month. Partial period interest is calculated on pro-rata basis. Interest is calculated and credited independently for each calendar month. Senior citizen accounts get additional 0.50% interest across all Savings Account categories.

title: Cheque Book Printing and Delivery Service
content: MCC Bank provides personalized cheque book printing with customer name and account details. Cheque book of 20 leaves is provided free of cost annually for Savings Bank account. Additional cheque books are charged at Rs. 4 per leaf for Savings Account and Rs. 5 per leaf for Current Account. Cheque book delivery is free at branch or can be sent by courier at Rs. 100 plus shipping charges. Expedited cheque book delivery is available within 3 business days for additional charge of Rs. 250. Cheque leaf serial number tracking prevents fraud. Customized cheque books with corporate logo available for business accounts.

title: Loan Against Deposit TAD Facility and Terms
content: MCC Bank offers Loan Against Deposit (TAD) where customer can borrow against existing Fixed or Recurring Deposit without premature closure. TAD loan amount is up to 85% of deposit value. Interest rate on TAD is 1% higher than deposit rate for loan amount up to Rs. 25 Lakhs and 2% higher for amount less than Rs. 25 Lakhs. Repayment tenure is up to maturity date of underlying deposit. TAD loan can be availed for emergency needs without processing charges. Monthly EMI payment option available for TAD loans. Original deposit continues to earn interest and can be renewed after TAD loan closure.

title: Credit Card Facility and EMI Conversion Options
content: MCC Bank credit cards are issued to eligible account holders based on income and creditworthiness. Credit limit ranges from Rs. 10,000 to Rs. 10,00,000. Annual card fee is Rs. 500 for primary card and Rs. 250 for supplementary cards, waived if annual spending exceeds Rs. 2,00,000. Interest rate on credit card outstanding balance is 18.00% per annum. Credit card offers 45 days interest-free credit period. EMI conversion option available for purchase amount above Rs. 5,000. EMI tenure options are 3, 6, 9, 12, and 24 months. Reward points earned at 1 point per Rs. 100 spent, redeemable for cash back or shopping vouchers.

title: Card Fraud Protection and Zero Liability Policy
content: MCC Bank implements zero liability policy for unauthorized card transactions if reported within 24 hours of noticing fraud. Customer is protected against fraudulent ATM withdrawals and online transactions. Card can be blocked immediately through mobile app, internet banking, or phone call. Replacement card is issued free of charge within 3-5 business days after blocking. ATM card skimming incidents are covered under bank's fraud protection insurance. Lost card should be reported immediately to block further transactions. Bank reverses fraudulent transaction amount within 7-10 business days after investigation. Card fraud alert SMS is sent within 1 minute of every transaction attempt.

title: International Money Transfer and Foreign Remittance
content: MCC Bank facilitates inbound and outbound international money transfers through correspondent banks. Inbound remittance from abroad is received in Rupees or foreign currency as per customer preference. Outbound remittance for resident customers is allowed up to resident annual limit as per FEMA regulations. International wire transfer charges are Rs. 750 plus applicable bank charges from correspondent banks. Foreign currency accounts can receive remittance in USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, and AED. Remittance documents required are beneficiary account details, IFSC code, SWIFT code, and proof of fund source. International fund transfer takes 3-5 business days for processing and settlement.

title: Forex Services and Currency Exchange Rates
content: MCC Bank provides forex services for currency exchange and foreign currency accounts. Exchange rate is updated daily based on RBI reference rate plus bank markup of 0.25%. Customer can exchange domestic currency for foreign currency for travel or business purposes. Exchange rate locked for 3 days from quotation. Forex exchange requires passport and travel itinerary for travel purposes. Maximum forex exchange limit per financial year is USD 250,000 equivalent under resident normal route. Forex exchange for business purposes requires business registration and business invoice. Online forex booking available through internet banking portal.

title: Locker Access Hours and Security Protocols
content: MCC Bank locker access is available during bank working hours from 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM on all working days. Safe deposit locker access requires unique key and entry in access register with customer signature. Dual custody protocol requires bank staff presence during locker opening. Video surveillance of locker facility is continuous for security. Lost locker key must be reported immediately and replacement key is provided at Rs. 1500 plus break open charges if necessary. Locker contents insurance is optional and can be taken for additional Rs. 500 per annum. Bank is not liable for loss of valuable items kept in locker unless insured. Locker can be transferred to nominee on death of locker holder.

title: Safe Custody and Valuables Storage Service
content: MCC Bank provides safe custody service for storing valuable documents and items. Safe custody charges are Rs. 100 per month or part thereof for documents kept in bank locker or strongroom. Valuable items like gold, jewelry, important documents, insurance policies, property deeds, and wills can be stored. Each item is assigned unique reference number and photographed for documentation. Itemized receipt is provided to customer with detailed description. Customer can retrieve items on demand with 24 hours notice. In case of death, authorized heirs can retrieve items with original death certificate and succession certificate. Safe custody items are covered under bank's general insurance.

title: Dispute Resolution and Customer Complaint Escalation
content: MCC Bank has formal complaint resolution procedure with multiple escalation levels. Level 1 complaint is registered at branch and resolved within 7 days. Level 2 escalation to Regional Manager is done if Level 1 resolution is unsatisfactory, resolved within 14 days. Level 3 escalation to General Manager for complex cases, resolved within 30 days. Level 4 complaint can be lodged with RBI Banking Ombudsman if internal resolution is not satisfactory. Customer receives unique complaint reference number and SMS confirmation on complaint registration. Complaint status can be tracked online on internet banking portal. Compensation or settlement is offered based on complaint investigation findings.

title: RBI Banking Ombudsman and External Resolution
content: MCC Bank customers can lodge complaint with RBI Banking Ombudsman if not satisfied with bank's internal resolution. Ombudsman complaint must be filed within 1 year of complaint to bank. Ombudsman office has no jurisdictional limit and covers all MCC Bank branches. Ombudsman complaint is filed online on rbidocs.rbi.org.in portal at no cost. Ombudsman decision is binding on bank and should be implemented within 30 days. RBI Ombudsman office is located in major cities across India. Average resolution time at Ombudsman office is 2-3 months. Ombudsman has power to award compensation up to Rs. 10,00,000 for customer grievance.

title: Consumer Protection Act and Customer Rights
content: MCC Bank operations are governed by Consumer Protection Act, 2019 providing comprehensive protection to consumers. Bank is liable for deficient service as per Act definition. Customer has right to lodge complaint under Consumer Protection Act at consumer court or commission. Limitation period for filing consumer complaint is 2 years from date of complaint event. Consumer court has jurisdiction for claim value up to Rs. 1 crore. Consumer protection includes protection against misleading advertisements and unfair trade practices. Bank must provide clear disclosure of all charges and terms before opening any product. Customer has right to access own records and obtain certified copies.

title: Personal Loan Features and Eligibility Criteria
content: MCC Bank Personal Loan is available to salaried and self-employed individuals with regular income source. Loan amount ranges from Rs. 50,000 to Rs. 10,00,000. Interest rate for Personal Loan is 14.00% per annum floating. Repayment tenure is 12 to 60 months. Minimum CIBIL score required is 600. Processing charges are 1.50% of loan amount up to Rs. 20 Lakhs. Personal loan requires one guarantor with income proof. Minimal documentation required including salary slips, identity proof, and address proof. Loan approval within 24 hours of document submission. Personal loan eligibility is subject to debt-to-income ratio not exceeding 50%.

title: Housing Loan Eligibility and Property Valuation
content: MCC Bank Housing Loan is available for purchase or construction of residential property. Loan amount up to Rs. 50 Lakhs for purchase and Rs. 30 Lakhs for construction. Interest rate for Housing Loan is 9.00% per annum floating. Repayment tenure up to 20 years. Borrower and co-borrower must be resident Indian citizens. Property must be in India and valued through bank-approved valuer. Maximum loan-to-value ratio is 80% of property value. Processing charges are 1% of loan amount. Legal documentation required including property title deed and property insurance. Disbursement after completion of legal formalities and property registration in bank's name.

title: Two-Wheeler Loan Rate and Terms
content: MCC Bank Two-Wheeler Loan is offered for purchase of new two-wheelers including motorcycles, scooters, and mopeds. Loan amount up to Rs. 2,00,000 based on two-wheeler cost and eligibility. Interest rate is 15.00% per annum for two-wheelers. Repayment tenure up to 5 years. Processing charges are 2% of loan amount. Two-wheeler must be insured comprehensively. Hypothecation of vehicle to bank is required as security. Loan eligibility requires minimum annual income of Rs. 2,00,000. Self-employed can apply with 2 years business ITR. Loan approval within 48 hours of document submission.

title: Home Improvement Loan and Renovation Benefits
content: MCC Bank Home Improvement Loan is offered for renovation, remodeling, or extension of existing residential property. Loan amount up to Rs. 20 Lakhs based on property valuation. Interest rate is 12.50% per annum floating. Repayment tenure up to 15 years. Property valuation by bank-approved valuer is mandatory. Maximum loan-to-value ratio is 60% of property value. Processing charges are 1.25% of loan amount. Home improvement work must be done by approved contractors. Loan disbursement is made in stages based on completion milestones. Completion certificate from contractor is required for final disbursement.

title: Agricultural Loan for Farming and Crop Production
content: MCC Bank Agricultural Loan is provided to farmers and agricultural entrepreneurs for crop production and farming activities. Loan amount up to Rs. 25 Lakhs depending on land holding and income. Interest rate is 10.50% per annum for crop loans. Repayment tenure up to 7 years. Collateral security is land or agricultural assets. Crop insurance is mandatory under Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana. Processing charges are 0.75% of loan amount. Margin requirement is 10% for crop loans. Post-harvest loan facility available at 10.00% for 6 months. Loan disbursement 7 days after approval and documentation.

title: Savings Account Features for Students and Youth
content: MCC Bank Youth Savings Account is specially designed for students below 25 years age. Minimum opening balance is only Rs. 100 for student accounts. No minimum balance requirement for account maintenance. Interest rate is 4.00% per annum for student savings accounts. Free cheque book facility with 20 leaves annually. Free demand draft and standing instruction facility. Debit card with lower annual fee of Rs. 50 per year. SMS alerts for all transactions at no cost. Free internet banking and mobile banking access. Account can be converted to regular Savings Account on turning 25 years age.

title: Senior Citizen Account Benefits and Concessions
content: MCC Bank Senior Citizen Savings Account is designed for individuals aged 60 years and above. Minimum opening balance is Rs. 500. Interest rate is 3.50% per annum with additional 0.50% concession for senior citizens. Monthly income pension account facility available with flexible deposit amount. Locker facility at 50% discount on locker rent. Additional Rs. 1500 term life insurance cover at no cost. Free cheque book facility throughout the year. No minimum balance penalty for senior citizen accounts. Dedicated senior citizen customer service desk at major branches. Free health check-up camps quarterly at branches.

title: Woman Empowerment Savings Account and Benefits
content: MCC Bank Woman Empowerment Savings Account is offered to women for financial independence. Minimum opening balance is only Rs. 500. Interest rate is 3.75% per annum for woman's account. Woman can avail loan up to 150% of deposit value for income generation activities. Microfinance group loans available at 12.00% interest rate. Business counseling and entrepreneurship training programs free of cost. Preferential treatment in priority sector lending guidelines. Locker facility at discounted rate of Rs. 1000 annually. Women-only customer service team available at branches. Monthly financial literacy workshops conducted exclusively for women account holders.

title: Recurring Deposit for Regular Savings and Forced Savings
content: MCC Bank Recurring Deposit is suitable for customers wanting forced monthly savings habit. Monthly installment amount minimum Rs. 100 and maximum Rs. 50,000. Repayment tenure from 3 months to 10 years. Interest rate is 6.75% for 1-2 years and 6.50% for 2-5 years. Standing instruction facility accepted for automatic monthly deduction. Nomination facility for beneficiary protection. Loan facility available up to 85% of RD balance. RD can be premature closed with 1% interest reduction. Senior citizen gets additional 0.50% interest on RD. Monthly installment flexibility allows pause of one month per calendar year.

title: Tax Saver Fixed Deposit and Section 80C Benefits
content: MCC Bank Tax Saver Fixed Deposit (TSFD) is specially designed to avail income tax deduction under Section 80C. Minimum deposit amount is Rs. 10,000 and maximum Rs. 50,00,000. Lock-in period is fixed at 5 years with no withdrawal allowed before maturity. Interest rate is 6.75% per annum for Tax Saver FD. Interest is taxable annually even if not withdrawn. Joint holding of TSFD is not permitted. Nomination facility is available. Deposit amount qualifies for 100% deduction under Section 80C up to Rs. 1,50,000 per financial year. Maturity proceeds can be reinvested in fresh TSFD for continued tax benefit.

title: Sweep Facility and Dynamic Interest Management
content: MCC Bank Sweep Facility automatically transfers excess balance from Savings Account to Fixed Deposit at better interest rate. Sweep limit can be set from Rs. 10,000 to any amount. When account balance exceeds sweep limit, excess amount is automatically converted to FD. When FD matures or balance falls below limit, FD amount is transferred back to Savings Account. Sweep frequency can be set as daily, weekly, or monthly. Interest rate on sweep FD is regular FD rate applicable for 30-45 days tenure. Sweep facility helps earn higher interest on idle balance. Sweep facility is absolutely free without any charges.

title: Emergency Loan Against Property Documentation
content: MCC Bank Emergency Loan Against Property is quick short-term credit against property documents. Loan amount up to Rs. 5,00,000 against property value. Interest rate is 18.00% per annum for emergency loans. Repayment tenure is 3 to 12 months only. Quick approval within 24 hours with minimal documentation. Property valuation by bank representative required. Loan can be availed for medical emergency, education, business crisis. Loan disbursement by cheque or fund transfer within 48 hours. No prepayment penalty if repaid before tenure. Personal guarantee required from borrower.

title: Seasonal Agricultural Operations SAO Loan Scheme
content: MCC Bank Seasonal Agricultural Operations (SAO) loan is provided for seasonal agricultural activities and input costs. Loan amount up to Rs. 10 Lakhs depending on acreage and crop type. Interest rate is 10.00% per annum floating. Repayment tenure up to 12 months aligned with crop cycle. Zero processing charges for loans up to Rs. 1 Lakh. Crop insurance is mandatory. Land records and crop details are required. Loan can be availed during pre-sowing season. Disbursement within 7 days of approval. Loan quantum can be increased annually based on loan performance history.

title: Prime Minister Mudra Loan PMMY Scheme
content: MCC Bank participates in Prime Minister Mudra Yojana (PMMY) for micro-enterprise development. Three categories of Mudra loans: Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishor (Rs. 50,001 to Rs. 5,00,000), and Tarun (Rs. 5,00,001 to Rs. 10,00,000). Interest rate depends on loan category and borrower profile, typically 12.50% to 14.50%. Repayment tenure from 3 to 7 years. Collateral-free loans for Shishu and Kishor categories. Unsecured personal guarantee required. Loan for micro-enterprises engaged in manufacturing, trading, or service sector. Credit guarantee insurance available under CGTMSE. Business plan required for loan approval.

title: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana PMJDY Account
content: MCC Bank offers Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) account for financial inclusion of unbanked population. PMJDY account can be opened with zero minimum balance. Basic account with limited transaction features. Free PMJDY RuPay debit card with 100,000 accident insurance. Free SMS alerts for all transactions. Overdraft facility up to Rs. 10,000 after 6 months of satisfactory operation. Interest rate on PMJDY savings is 2.50% per annum. Accidental death insurance of Rs. 1,00,000 included free. Online banking facility to access account. PMJDY account can be upgraded to regular Savings Account anytime.

title: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana Account SSY for Girls
content: MCC Bank offers Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) account for girl child education and marriage fund accumulation. Account can be opened for girl child below 10 years age. Account holder becomes account-holder on attaining 18 years and can operate. Minimum annual deposit is Rs. 250 and maximum Rs. 1,50,000. Account maturity is 21 years from opening date. Interest rate is 7.60% per annum for FY 2025-26. Interest accrued is tax-free under Section 80C. Withdrawal allowed after 50% balance or 18 years, whichever is earlier. Nominee facility for beneficiary protection. Account can be opened only at bank branch.

title: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme SCSS Government Scheme
content: MCC Bank offers Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) for senior citizens above 60 years. Account opening is restricted to senior citizens aged 60 years and above. Minimum deposit Rs. 1,000 and maximum Rs. 30,00,000. Account maturity is 5 years from opening date. Interest rate is 8.20% per annum for FY 2025-26 payable quarterly. Investment tax-free under Section 80C up to Rs. 1,50,000 limit. Interest earned is taxable as per income tax slab. Account can be extended for another 3 years. Monthly, quarterly, or annual interest payment options available. Withdrawal rules: No withdrawal for first year, maximum 50% from year 2.

title: Kisan Credit Card KCC for Agricultural Production
content: MCC Bank issues Kisan Credit Card (KCC) for agricultural credit requirements. KCC credit limit based on land holding and crop pattern up to Rs. 3,00,000. Interest rate is 4% per annum with government concession if timely repayment. Floating rate after concession period. Repayment tenure flexible from 12 to 36 months. Crop insurance mandatory under Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana. KCC valid for 3 years with annual renewal. KCC can be used to procure agricultural inputs and for crop production. Zero processing charges for KCC up to Rs. 1 Lakh. Overdraft facility available up to KCC limit sanctioned.

title: NABARD National Bank Agriculture Rural Development
content: MCC Bank acts as lending institution for NABARD-sponsored agricultural development schemes. NABARD funds subsidized agricultural loans for farmers' groups and cooperatives. Interest rate with NABARD concession is significantly lower for eligible schemes. Repayment tenure up to 10 years for long-term agricultural loans. Group lending model under NABARD promotes farmer cooperation. Subsidies and concessions directly credited by NABARD. Farmer training and capacity building supported by NABARD funds. MCC Bank coordinates with NABARD for fund disbursement and monitoring. Eligible farmer groups can borrow up to Rs. 10,00,000 under NABARD schemes.

title: SIDBI Small Industries Development Bank Participation
content: MCC Bank as SIDBI participating bank provides MSME lending under SIDBI schemes. SIDBI provides refinance at concessional rates for eligible MSME loans. Credit guarantee and credit enhancement schemes available. MSME definition as per Government of India norms: Manufacturing - turnover up to Rs. 5 Crore; Service - turnover up to Rs. 2 Crore. SIDBI fund availability for working capital and fixed asset funding. Collateral-free lending up to Rs. 1 Crore under SIDBI Credit Guarantee Scheme. Business development services and counseling provided. Technology upgradation schemes supported through SIDBI funds.

title: Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation DICGC
content: MCC Bank deposits are insured under Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC) scheme. Insurance coverage of Rs. 5,00,000 per depositor per bank covering principal and interest. DICGC insurance protection applies to all bank deposits including Savings, Current, Fixed, and Recurring Deposits. In case of bank failure, DICGC ensures depositor funds up to maximum Rs. 5,00,000 per bank. Joint account deposits are insured separately. Each account holder is covered individually. DICGC claim settlement within 30 days of bank failure notification. All MCC Bank deposit customers are automatically protected under DICGC.

title: Reserve Bank of India RBI Monetary Policy Rate
content: MCC Bank interest rates are aligned with RBI Monetary Policy Rate (Repo Rate). Current Repo Rate is 6.50% per annum. All MCC Bank lending rates are linked to MCLR (Marginal Cost of Funds-based Lending Rate) which moves with RBI Repo Rate. Deposits and loan rates adjusted within 30 days of RBI rate change announcement. RBI rate decisions made bi-monthly on first Wednesday of April and October. Rate changes are passed to customers through notifications and website updates. Fixed deposit rates remain constant as per contracted rate. Floating rate deposits and loans adjusted every quarter after rate reset.

title: Cash Reserve Ratio CRR and Statutory Liquidity Ratio SLR
content: MCC Bank maintains Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) of 4.50% with RBI as per regulatory requirements. CRR is amount of deposits kept with RBI earning fixed rate. Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) of 18% maintained in government securities and gold. SLR assets are kept as ready liquid resources to meet customer demands. SLR maintenance ensures bank stability and systemic safety. CRR and SLR combined form bank's statutory reserve requirements. Non-compliance with CRR/SLR requirements attracts penalty from RBI. RBI may adjust CRR/SLR to regulate money supply in economy. Higher CRR means less money available for customer lending.

title: Priority Sector Lending PSL Achievement and Targets
content: MCC Bank achieves Priority Sector Lending (PSL) target of 75% as per RBI guidelines. PSL includes lending to agriculture, small industries, MSME, education, and housing below Rs. 30 Lakhs. Agriculture advances target is 18% of net credit. MSME target is 15% of net credit. Housing loans for purchase up to Rs. 30 Lakhs count as PSL. Small traders and micro enterprises covered under PSL guidelines. Non-achievement of PSL target requires fund transfer to designated fund. Bank publishes quarterly PSL achievements in financial disclosures. PSL lending promotes financial inclusion and development.

title: Basel III Capital Adequacy Ratio CAR Compliance
content: MCC Bank maintains Capital Adequacy Ratio (CAR) above 10.50% as per Basel III norms. CAR comprises Tier-1 capital (core capital) and Tier-2 capital (supplementary capital). Tier-1 capital includes paid-up capital, reserves, and retained earnings. Tier-2 capital includes subordinated borrowings and revaluation reserves. Higher CAR indicates stronger financial position and lending capacity. Minimum CAR requirement protects depositors and ensures stability. Basel III implementation strengthens banking system resilience. Bank raises capital through rights issue or bonus shares to maintain CAR above minimum.

title: Net Stable Funding Ratio NSFR and Liquidity Coverage
content: MCC Bank maintains Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) above 100% as per international standards. NSFR measures bank's ability to meet funding needs over one-year horizon. Stable funding sources include deposits, equity capital, and long-term borrowings. Available stable funding divided by required funding determines NSFR ratio. High NSFR indicates lower liquidity risk. Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) measures short-term liquidity buffer for 30-day stress period. High-quality liquid assets maintained to cover outflows. Bank stress tests NSFR and LCR monthly for contingency planning.

title: Asset Quality Non-Performing Assets NPA and Provisioning
content: MCC Bank maintains Net NPA ratio below 1.50% indicating sound asset quality. Non-Performing Assets (NPA) are loans where payment is overdue by 90 days or more. NPAs are classified as Substandard (12-24 months overdue), Doubtful (24+ months overdue), or Loss (3+ years overdue). Provision coverage ratio of 70% maintained for NPA cover. Higher provision reduces net NPA and book value of assets. NPA ratio monitored quarterly and disclosed in financial statements. Collection efforts and recovery actions minimize NPA growth. Customer facing stress is accommodated through restructuring and loan modification schemes.

title: Digital Payment Infrastructure and NEFT-RTGS IMPS
content: MCC Bank provides integrated payment infrastructure supporting NEFT, RTGS, IMPS, and UPI. IMPS (Immediate Payment Service) enables instant fund transfer up to Rs. 2,00,000 24x7. UPI (Unified Payments Interface) allows mobile number-based fund transfer. NACH (National Automated Clearing House) enables bulk recurring payments. Digital payments integration with National Payment Corporation of India (NPCI). Payment gateway integration for online merchant transactions. BHIM and Rupay card integration for digital payment ecosystem participation.

title: Branch Network and ATM Accessibility Across Karnataka
content: MCC Bank operates 85 branches across Karnataka serving Tier-1, Tier-2, and Tier-3 cities. Major branch presence in Mangalore, Bangalore, Udupi, Puttur, Karkala, Surathkal, and Belgaum. ATM network of 150+ machines accessible in all major locations. Any branch banking facility allows deposit and withdrawal from any branch. Core Banking System (CBS) integration enables real-time transaction visibility. Video conferencing facility available at head office and select branches. Dedicated grievance redressal centers at regional offices. Mobile branch vehicle for remote area banking access.

title: Customer Service Quality and Response Time Standards
content: MCC Bank commits to prompt customer service with 24-hour response time for complaints. Customer service toll-free number 1800-425-1111 available 24x7. Email support available at help@mccbank.in with 2-hour response commitment. Live chat support on website and mobile app for real-time assistance. Branch walk-in customers served within 30 minutes average waiting time. Customer satisfaction survey conducted quarterly to measure service quality. NPS (Net Promoter Score) target of 50+ maintained through service excellence. Customer feedback incorporated in training programs for staff development.

title: Employee Training and Financial Literacy Programs
content: MCC Bank conducts regular employee training on product knowledge and customer service. Financial literacy programs conducted quarterly at branch level for customer education. Programs cover topics like savings, investments, credit, insurance, and tax planning. Customer awareness campaigns on government schemes like PMJDY, SSY, SCSS. School education programs on financial inclusion and cooperative banking principles. Senior citizen workshops on digital banking, fund transfer, and security practices. Women entrepreneurship programs covering business planning, loan application, and fund management. Free webinars conducted on financial planning and investment strategies.

title: Corporate Social Responsibility CSR and Community Development
content: MCC Bank implements CSR initiatives for community development and social welfare. Education sponsorship program assists 500+ underprivileged students annually for school and college education. Healthcare camps provide free medical checkups in rural areas. Skill development training programs for unemployed youth in partnership with government agencies. Environmental conservation initiatives including tree plantation drives and water harvesting projects. Support to farmers through agricultural training and best practice dissemination. Women self-help group support with funding and training. Disaster relief programs providing financial assistance during natural calamities.

title: Financial Year Calendar and Account Maintenance Period
content: MCC Bank financial year runs from April 1 to March 31 annually. Account year for interest calculation aligns with calendar year January to December. Savings Bank account statement issued on 30th of March or 31st December accordingly. Half-yearly statement issued on September 30. Quarterly interest crediting on March 31, June 30, September 30, December 31. Annual General Meeting held within 4 months after financial year closure. Dividend distribution declared based on annual profit and member decision. Statutory reserve fund maintenance as per RBI guidelines. Public disclosure of financial results on bank website within timeline mandated by RBI.

title: Code of Conduct and Ethical Banking Practices
content: MCC Bank follows strict Code of Conduct for ethical banking practices and transparency. Anti-corruption policy ensures no unethical practices in lending or customer dealings. Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) compliance mandatory for all transactions. Interest rate disclosure and transparency in product pricing. Fair lending practices ensuring credit availability to eligible customers without discrimination. Customer data privacy protection under strict data security protocols. Whistleblower protection policy for reporting unethical practices confidentially. External audit by independent auditors ensures financial statement integrity and credibility.

title: Technology and Cybersecurity Measures Implementation
content: MCC Bank invests in latest cybersecurity technology and data protection measures. SSL/TLS encryption for all online transactions and data transmission. Multi-layer firewall and intrusion detection systems against cyber threats. Regular security audits by external cyber security experts quarterly. Data center backup and disaster recovery systems for business continuity. Customer data encrypted with 256-bit AES encryption standards. Cyber insurance policy covering data breach and financial losses. Employee training on cyber awareness and data handling practices. Compliance with RBI cybersecurity guidelines and standards.

title: Future Growth Plan and Digital Transformation Strategy
content: MCC Bank plans expansion to 150 branches by 2030 covering tier-2 and tier-3 cities. Investment in fintech and digital banking platform enhancement targeted. AI-powered customer service chatbot deployment planned for 24x7 support. Mobile-first banking experience with voice-activated transactions in planning stage. Payment aggregator platform for merchant and retail customer transactions. Blockchain technology exploration for secure transaction settlement. Open banking API ecosystem development for partner integration. Green banking initiatives and sustainable finance products planned.

title: Partnership and Strategic Alliances for Service Enhancement
content: MCC Bank partners with NPCI for digital payment ecosystem participation. Partnership with CIBIL for credit information report integration. Tie-up with insurance companies for bancassurance products and solutions. Technology partnerships with leading software providers for digital infrastructure. Correspondent banking relationships with major international banks. Partnership with government agencies for social welfare scheme implementation. Collaboration with fintech companies for innovation in banking services. Joint ventures for specialized financing products and market expansion.

title: Contact Information and Office Locations
content: MCC Bank Head Office: Hampankatta, Mangalore - 575001, Karnataka. Phone: 0824-2221900, Fax: 0824-2223600. Email: info@mccbank.in, gm@mccbank.in, chairman@mccbank.in. Regional offices in Bangalore, Kochi, Pune, and Delhi. Customer Care Toll-Free: 1800-425-1111 available 24x7. WhatsApp Support: 7090701104 for NRE account queries. Website: www.mccbank.in for online banking and service information. Official social media handles on Facebook, Twitter, and YouTube for updates and announcements. Branch locator tool on website to find nearest branch and ATM.
